data.title: PTY allocator wedge: new terminal surfaces created but PTY never attaches ('Terminal surface not found' on read-screen indefinitely) at low surface counts; survives app restart

data.body: Repro evidence from the overtone backlog-clear run (2026-06-04/05, workspace:3): 5+ consecutive zombies across pane:121 AND pane:110 at ~10 total surfaces (below the known ~15/pane threshold). 'c11 new-surface' returns OK with a ref; the surface renders in 'c11 tree'; but read-screen errors 'Terminal surface not found' forever (waited 15s+). Existing surfaces unaffected. App restart yielded exactly ONE successful attach, then re-wedged on the next create ~60s later. Workaround that held for hours: reuse finished delegator surfaces (/exit the idle claude, launch into the freed shell) and suspend auto-close. Snapshots from the wedge window: 01KT9XFPWVE303QBP6J8PK4TW3, 01KT9YFCBD58J1ZM80Z6N1HGJ8. Binary: c11 0.50.0 (107).
